data.description:
# Refresh exact-pinned dev tooling: zizmor + ruff

Bump two exact `==` pins in `pyproject.toml`'s `[dev]` extra to current, clean releases.

## Why
- **zizmor 1.27.0 is YANKED** for GHSA-f42p-wjw5-97qh (Moderate, CWE-532): 1.27.0 prints
  GitHub tokens (`GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN`/`ZIZMOR_GITHUB_TOKEN`) to stderr under `-v`/debug
  logging. Only 1.27.0 is affected; fix is 1.28.0. rebar's `make lint` invokes `zizmor
  release.yml` with no verbose flag, so there is no live exposure, but pinning exactly to a
  security-yanked release is fragile (a stricter resolver / pruned cache can fail installs).
- **ruff 0.15.19 -> 0.15.22**: routine patch refresh to stay current on the format/lint pin
  (exact-pinned for local<->CI parity). No yank/security; grouped here as a low-risk sibling.

## Plan
1. `pyproject.toml`: `zizmor==1.27.0` -> `1.28.0`; refresh the pin comment that names 1.27.0.
2. `pyproject.toml`: `ruff==0.15.19` -> `0.15.22`.
3. Reinstall dev extra so the worktree venv has the new tools.
4. Run `make lint` — if the newer ruff reformats anything, apply `ruff format` and include the
   diff (format-check must be green in CI). Run `make typecheck`.
5. One commit, `rebar-ticket` trailer + DCO sign-off; push to Gerrit; land after
   `LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both pins updated to 1.28.0 / 0.15.22 with the stale "1.27.0" comment corrected.
- `make lint` and `make typecheck` pass locally with the bumped tools.
- Any ruff-format churn from the bump is applied so CI format-check is green.
- Change lands on `main` through Gerrit with both required votes; no other files touched.
data.title: Bump zizmor 1.27.0->1.28.0 (yanked/GHSA) and ruff 0.15.19->0.15.22
